description: Waterfall Claude OS — canonical catalog of every skill across the Waterfall Technologies platform. Source of truth lives in the Waterfall-Claude-OS repo (assets/global/registry.json), which also holds a full mirror of every skill (.claude/skills + .agents/skills) and the portable waterfall-skills bundle. `installed_in` is where a skill physically ships (loaded by Claude Code from that repo's .claude/skills or .agents/skills); `applies_to` is where it is relevant. OS-core skills ship in every repo. Any app-repo catalog (e.g. cairo-ai-pro/app-assets/global) is a superseded mirror.

skills.name: active-risk-monitoring
skills.description: Build the “active insurance” moat — continuous signal ingestion (IoT, telematics, scans, weather) → a live risk score → proactive prevention alerts that lower loss frequency, plus telematics onboarding and transparent dynamic pricing.
skills.integration_notes: The loss-frequency moat (Coalition/Hippo/Root patterns). Monitoring agent routes through the Super Agent (HAIKU for signal classification, SONNET/OPUS for narrative); risk math is deterministic. Feeds insurance-unit-economics (loss ratio) and dynamic pricing. Sequence Phase 3 after claims-automation + insurance-unit-economics. Diff 5.
skills.recommended_usage: When building IoT/sensor integration, telematics onboarding, a risk dashboard, prevention alerts, or usage-based/dynamic pricing.

skills.name: add-route
skills.description: Add a TanStack Router route the safe way — scaffold under src/routes/ and regenerate routeTree.gen.ts with the generator, never by hand.
skills.integration_notes: Stack-specific to TanStack Start file-based routing; not portable to react-router apps (waterfall-nexus).
skills.recommended_usage: Adding a page, nested/layout route, or API/server route in cairo-ai-pro.

skills.name: cairo-global-asset-manager
skills.description: Ingest, classify, integrate, and maintain global assets (agents, skills, tools, workflows, templates) under app-assets/global/ and this registry.
skills.integration_notes: Admin-only. Manages the canonical OS catalog, which now lives in Waterfall-Claude-OS/assets/global/ (the cairo-ai-pro/app-assets/global copy is a superseded mirror). The waterfall-os skill is the platform-facing entry point that references it.
skills.recommended_usage: When an admin adds an external asset to the platform or audits the global asset library.

skills.name: ci-cd-conventions
skills.description: Add or modify GitHub Actions workflows compatible with the Bun + Cloudflare Workers edge build.
skills.integration_notes: Cairo CI specifics (Bun, Cloudflare). Generalize before reuse in npm/vitest repos like waterfall-tech-command.
skills.recommended_usage: Editing .github/workflows or adding lint/typecheck/build/preview jobs in cairo-ai-pro.

skills.name: claims-automation
skills.description: Agentic claims pipeline — conversational FNOL → ML triage → AI damage estimate → instant payout — Super-Agent-routed, with the invariant that the fraud/deepfake gate runs BEFORE any auto-payout. Audit exits non-zero on HIGH (CI gate).
skills.integration_notes: Orchestrates (does not replace) insurance-claims-ux (UX) and fraud-deepfake-guard (media gate). Load-bearing invariant: no auto-payout path without a preceding fraud gate — the audit fails CI on violation. The margin lever measured by insurance-unit-economics automation rate. Diff 4.
skills.recommended_usage: When building a file-a-claim flow, claims console, FNOL bot, auto-adjudication, or instant-payout path.

skills.name: github-integration-authoring
skills.description: Add or modify a GitHub action/trigger in Cairo's GitHub integration (src/lib/integrations/github) — REST actions, event triggers, schemas, manifest.
skills.recommended_usage: Implementing new REST-backed GitHub actions or event triggers in cairo-ai-pro.

skills.name: github-webhook-security
skills.description: Implement or review inbound webhook handlers — verify X-Hub-Signature-256, process repository events safely.
skills.integration_notes: Mechanics (HMAC verify, constant-time compare) are portable; paths reference cairo /api/public/* routes.
skills.recommended_usage: Adding webhook receivers or verifying signatures in cairo-ai-pro.

skills.name: insurance-unit-economics
skills.description: Instrument the six embedded-MGA unit-economics metrics — loss ratio, combined ratio, take/ceding commission, CAC & LTV/CAC, attach rate, automation rate — as live, A/B-testable dashboards. Scaffolds a typed metrics module + dashboard; audits whether each KPI is computed.
skills.integration_notes: Instrument-first: ships before the agentic features so claims-automation and active-risk-monitoring have a scoreboard. KPI math is deterministic; any narrative/anomaly/projection routes through the Super Agent. Derives from the policy/claim event log emitted by embedded-insurance-sdk. Diff 2.
skills.recommended_usage: When building partner/admin dashboards, before a board/capacity-partner review, or whenever asked about loss ratio, combined ratio, attach rate, take rate, LTV/CAC, or automation rate.

skills.name: performance-optimizer
skills.description: Static performance profiling — Supabase over-fetch/N+1, missing limits, TanStack Query caching, bundle/latency smells — with a before/after report and score.
skills.integration_notes: perf-scan.sh is git-root-relative and grep-based; React + Supabase oriented. Installed on the React-heavy apps.
skills.recommended_usage: When something feels slow, before a release, or when asked to profile/optimize.

skills.name: preview-doctor
skills.description: Diagnose and fix Lovable preview failures for a TanStack Start app — Node built-ins in the client graph, stale routeTree.gen.ts, asset/import hazards.
skills.integration_notes: Specific to TanStack Start + Lovable dev preview.
skills.recommended_usage: When the Lovable preview misbehaves or before handing a build to preview.

skills.name: release-and-deploy
skills.description: Understand the deploy target and secret promotion — Cloudflare Workers (wrangler.jsonc), env/secret promotion, published vs preview URLs.
skills.recommended_usage: Configuring deploys or promoting secrets between dev and prod in cairo-ai-pro.

skills.name: repo-hygiene
skills.description: OS-core. Maintain GitHub repo governance — CODEOWNERS, PR templates, branch protection, conventional PRs, and (for Lovable-synced repos) the two-way sync caveats.
skills.integration_notes: OS-core: installed in every Waterfall repo. Generalized from cairo-only (moved from .agents/skills to .claude/skills); Lovable-sync section is marked as applying only to Lovable-synced repos.
skills.recommended_usage: Setting up repository governance or advising on PR/branch workflow.

skills.name: security-monitor
skills.description: Static security sweep — RLS coverage, SSRF guards, secret leakage, permissive policies, webhook signature checks, browser-hostile imports. Exits non-zero on HIGH/CRITICAL.
skills.integration_notes: scan.sh is git-root-relative and no-ops on absent dirs (supabase/migrations, src/routes), so it ships safely to any repo with app source. Usable as a CI gate.
skills.recommended_usage: Before a release, after adding a connector/migration, or when asked to scan/check security.

skills.name: supabase-feature
skills.description: Add a Supabase-backed feature securely — scaffold a migration with RLS enabled + owner-scoped policies, plus a matching authenticated server accessor (TanStack server function or Deno Edge Function).
skills.integration_notes: Generalized: shared RLS migration (new-migration.sh is git-root-relative) plus two server-accessor variants — TanStack server fn (cairo-ai-pro) and Deno Edge Function (Lovable-Cloud apps like waterfall-nexus).
skills.recommended_usage: Adding a new table or any server code that reads/writes user data on a Supabase app.

skills.name: superagent-conformance
skills.description: Enforce THE ONE RULE — every AI call routes through the Super Agent. Static sweep for raw provider-API fetches, direct provider SDK use, hardcoded model strings, and manual token caps in app code; refactor to the approved superAgent/useAgent/shim patterns. Exits non-zero on HIGH.
skills.integration_notes: scan.sh is git-root-relative and no-ops cleanly (exit 0) when there's no src/. The model-access engine is allowlisted by default (src/agent, any superAgent file, src/lib/ai, *.gen.*); a repo extends it via .claude/skills/superagent-conformance/allowlist.txt — cairo-ai-pro ships one scoping its model-router/billing/BYOK/model-studio domain so only true consumer-app violations flag. Usable as a CI gate. Follow-up: install in waterfall-nexus (out of session scope).
skills.recommended_usage: Reviewing AI/LLM code, before a release, after adding an AI feature or connector, or when asked to check conformance / the one rule / model strings / token caps.

skills.name: task-planner
skills.description: OS-core. Decompose any goal into a dependency-aware plan and route each subtask to a skill that actually exists. The orchestration layer of the Waterfall Claude OS.
skills.integration_notes: OS-core: installed in every Waterfall repo. Routing reflects each repo's installed set via list-skills.sh.
skills.recommended_usage: Multi-step or multi-domain requests, or whenever a request spans more than one skill.

skills.name: tool-authoring
skills.description: Author, register, and wire a new Cairo AI tool (AITool interface) in src/lib/ai/tools/registry.ts.
skills.integration_notes: Tied to cairo-ai-pro's AI tool registry. Tools must route AI through the Super Agent, never call models directly.
skills.recommended_usage: Adding or modifying a tool exposed to Cairo agents.

skills.name: waterfall-os
skills.description: OS-core. The Waterfall Claude OS entry point — platform contract (route every AI call through the Super Agent), where this registry lives, installed skill set, and the add/unify-a-skill workflow.
skills.integration_notes: OS-core: installed in every Waterfall repo. Source of truth for the platform contract and the catalog pointer.
skills.recommended_usage: First skill to consult when landing in any Waterfall repo.
